commandPalette.title: Command Palette
layout.content.designRule: The left column now holds section switching and supporting context together, while the right column stays focused on the primary workspace.
layout.inspector.title: Context details
layout.inspector.description: A compact summary stays visible while you work in the main content area.
layout.section.workspace.title: Simplified two-column workspace
layout.section.workspace.content: Section switching and supporting context now live in the same left column, so the workspace feels calmer and the main surface has more room for actual tasks.
layout.section.workspace.insight: This layout removes the extra icon rail, reduces visual fragmentation, and keeps navigation and context in one stable place.
layout.section.workspace.panelDescriptio: The left column can hold navigation, shortcuts, filters, or summaries that support the selected section.
layout.section.workspace.panelBlockBody: Keep this column focused on context, not full task execution. It should support the main area, not compete with it.
layout.section.workspace.panelHintBody: Selecting a section tab updates the left column while leaving the main workspace stable.
layout.section.workspace.inspectorBody: Prioritize navigation summaries, recent items, and lightweight metadata in the inspector.
layout.section.appearance.title: Theme and presentation controls
layout.section.appearance.content: Appearance settings belong in the supporting panel so users can preview visual changes without losing their place in the main content region.
layout.section.appearance.insight: Light and dark themes should remain consistent across the navigation column and core workspace by relying on shared semantic tokens.
layout.section.appearance.panelDescripti: Place palette, density, and visual preference tools here to keep presentation controls together.
layout.section.appearance.panelBlockBody: Global styles belong in packages/css, with light tokens in light.css and dark tokens in dark.css so every desktop surface stays aligned.
layout.section.appearance.panelHintBody: A user can switch here, inspect the supporting guidance, and still keep the actual working canvas visible in the main column.
layout.section.appearance.inspectorBody: Use this area for contrast notes, token summaries, or visual QA hints related to the active theme.
layout.section.activity.title: Operational signals and recent events
layout.section.activity.content: Activity views work well in the simplified two-column model because the left column can summarize events while the main area stays free for drill-down or recovery workflows.
layout.section.activity.insight: Use the left column for recent events and status buckets, then reserve the main area for logs, diagnostics, and deeper investigation.
layout.section.activity.panelDescription: Recent updates, health indicators, and notifications can stay visible without overwhelming the central workspace.
layout.section.activity.panelBlockBody: Surface what changed recently, what needs attention, and which actions are safe to defer.
layout.section.activity.panelHintBody: This is the right place for compact status context before the user opens richer details in the main content area.
layout.section.activity.inspectorBody: Keep summaries concise here so the main content column can focus on traceability and remediation.
toast.error.shortcutRestoreDescription: The shortcut may be out of sync. Please restart the app or try again.
